steps.title: Identify the conservation principle
steps.content: When mixing solutions with different concentrations, the total amount of pure salt is conserved—that is, salt from solution 1 + salt from solution 2 = salt in final mixture. Convert each percentage to decimal form: 2% = 0.02, 5% = 0.05, and 3% = 0.03.

steps.title: Set up the conservation equation
steps.content:
For each solution, amount of salt = concentration (as decimal) × volume:
  • From $x$ liters of 2% solution: $0.02x$ liters of salt
  • From $y$ liters of 5% solution: $0.05y$ liters of salt
  • From $(x + y)$ liters of 3% solution: $0.03(x + y)$ liters of salt
By conservation, these amounts must be equal:
$$0.02x + 0.05y = 0.03(x + y)$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
